decision_summary: The chip temperature (89.26°C) exceeds the limit (85.0°C) by ~4.26°C. Previous iterations likely exhausted single-variable gains on conductivity or size. Per the strategy note to try a 'coordinated multi-variable change', I will simultaneously increase the heat spreader's conductivity and width. This combines material enhancement with geometric expansion to improve lateral heat spreading more aggressively than either lever alone.

changes.reason: Increase conductivity by 20% (within 2.0x bound) to reduce internal thermal resistance of the spreader, facilitating faster lateral heat distribution.
Increase spreader width to the maximum allowed bound (1.0m) to maximize the surface area available for heat dissipation towards the side boundaries.

expected_effects: Significant reduction in chip peak temperature due to improved lateral spreading efficiency.
More uniform temperature profile across the top layer.
Potential closure of the 4.26°C violation margin.
risk_notes: Diminishing returns: If the bottleneck is vertical conduction through the base plate rather than lateral spreading, these changes may yield smaller-than-expected improvements.
Geometric constraint: The spreader width is now at the domain limit; future iterations must focus on vertical geometry or base material properties if further improvement is needed.
